name: Sweet Pepper Soup ("Acquacotta")
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Sweet red or green peppers
recipeInstructions:
The literal translation of acquacotta is "cooked water". This Tuscan soup is internationally famous.
1. Heat the olive oil in a large heavy pan and sauté the onions until soft and transparent. Remove the seeds and cores from the peppers and cut the flesh into strips. Add them to the pan along with the celery and tomatoes, seasoning to taste with salt. Cook over a brisk heat for 30 minutes.
2. Pour in the boiling water, check the seasoning, and boil for 5 minutes.
3. Beat the eggs until smooth with a pinch of salt and stir in the grated Parmesan cheese.
4. Pour the soup into a heated tureen and quickly stir in the egg mixture. Ladle into soup bowls over slices of toasted bread. Stirring the egg mixture directly into the boiling soup is an alternative if you are concerned about the eggs. Make sure to pour the eggs in slowly while stirring so they don't form a clump.
